reasoning: The linear trend suggests a reduction of roughly 2.5B per day in reserve balances (driven by ongoing QT). The January 14 print of 3.06 trillion represents a significant local spike up from 2.85T in late December, likely due to typical year-end/early-January liquidity dynamics. Mid-January tax receipts often cause the Treasury General Account to swell, which correspondingly drains reserve balances. Mean reversion combined with the linear downward drift makes it unlikely that reserves will exceed 3.06 trillion by Jan 25 (P=0.37). For the longer February 17 horizon, higher variance moderates the downward expectations, shifting the probability closer to the 0.50 uncertainty baseline (P=0.45) as instructed.
